reasoning: Aleksandra Goryachkina's rating on the July 1, 2025 FIDE list was 2533. To gain 1% in Elo means an increase of over 25 points. Elite chess players (K=10) rarely gain 25+ points over short periods, as this requires consistently dominating similarly-rated opponents or greatly outperforming expectations against lower/higher ones. Recently, she was eliminated in Round 2 of the 2025 FIDE Women's World Cup, suggesting a slight rating decline moving into August. Consequently, her August 3 baseline will likely be equal to or slightly lower than 2533, still demanding a massive ~25 point climb to reach a 1% increase. FIDE only officially publishes rating lists at the start of each month, making an increase by August 10 effectively impossible since it perfectly overlaps with the August 1 list. By September, gaining ~25 points remains highly improbable without uncharacteristically stringing together multiple major tournament victories. For November and January, the possibility increases slightly to 12% and 18%. She received a wildcard to the Grand Swiss Open section (likely October/November), where playing higher-rated 2600+ opponents offers a mathematical pathway to gain rating points. Even so, scoring highly enough against super-GMs to net 25 points would require a top-tier career performance, keeping the overall probability firmly low.
